text:
This is Daily Brief World on RadioAgent.

Alright folks, let's talk about what actually matters today. Oil just surged back to about a hundred dollars a barrel because explosions have been reported on two more foreign ships in the Gulf. Two more. We're watching a shipping lane that carries roughly a fifth of the world's oil supply turn into something out of a Tom Clancy novel, and I gotta be honest, the market reaction feels almost calm compared to what this could become. Insurance premiums on tankers going through those waters are already astronomical. Every dollar on that barrel price ripples through everything, your groceries, your heating bill, the cost of running the T which already feels like a luxury experience you didn't ask for.

Meanwhile, speaking of explosions of a different kind, I hear the sixth floor of the MIT Media Lab is still buzzing from HARD MODE even though that hackathon technically wrapped days ago. Cyrus Clarke apparently built things during the event AND built the website. Overachievers in this town, I swear.

But back to those shipping lanes. If a third incident happens this week, we're not talking about oil prices anymore. We're talking about something else entirely. Stay with me.
